id.description: The ID assigned to a test case

documentation.description: A human-readable description of what the test case exercises

input.description: The environment, installed modules, and simulated source responses for a test

identityType.description: The identity type the chain is parameterized on. One of 'AwsCredentialsIdentity' or 'TokenIdentity'. Defaults to 'AwsCredentialsIdentity' when omitted.

installedModules.description:
The credential modules present / discoverable by the plugin mechanism. This is the modularity dimension: a StandardProvider slot only has a registered provider when its owning module is installed. Members are language-neutral module tokens; each SDK maps a token to its own artifact (e.g. 'sts' -> the SDK's STS credentials module). The following mapping from token to the slots it claims is normative for the test harness:
- core (always implicitly present): ENVIRONMENT, SHARED_CONFIG, PROFILE_STATIC_KEYS, PROFILE_SESSION_KEYS, PROFILE_CREDENTIAL_PROCESS (plus any language-specific ambient sources such as JVM system properties)
- sts: WEB_IDENTITY_TOKEN_ENV, PROFILE_ASSUME_ROLE, PROFILE_WEB_IDENTITY
- sso: PROFILE_SSO_SESSION, PROFILE_LEGACY_SSO
- ecs: ECS_CONTAINER
- imds: EC2_INSTANCE_METADATA
- login: PROFILE_LOGIN
When omitted, the harness MUST assume only the 'core' module is installed.

customProviders.description: Third-party or service-team ChainIdentityProvider implementations discovered alongside the standard providers. Used to exercise Before/After positioning and assembly-time validation.

name.description: The provider's canonical name(). Two providers sharing a name is an assembly-time hard error.

slot.description: The StandardProvider enum value referenced by the constraint (e.g. 'ENVIRONMENT', 'PROFILE_SSO_SESSION', 'EC2_INSTANCE_METADATA').

supportsIdentityType.description: Whether this provider registers a resolver for the requested identityType. When false, setup() returns without registering (the provider is skipped). Defaults to true.

present.description: Whether the provider detects its source as present during setup(). When false, the provider registers nothing and assembly falls through. Defaults to true.

terminal.description: Whether the provider registers via addTerminalResolver() (true) or addResolver() (false). Defaults to true.

response.description: What the provider's resolver returns at resolution time. Either resolved credentials/token or an error.

error.description: The failure the resolver raises at resolution time

clientIdentityResolver.description: An IdentityResolver supplied directly on the client builder. When present, it takes precedence and the assembled chain is NOT installed; every other input is ignored.

profileNameOverride.description: The client-specified profile name override. When null/omitted, the default resolution order applies (AWS_PROFILE, aws.profile, then 'default').

preloadedConfigFile.description: A caller-supplied, already-parsed AWS config/credentials file. When present, the SHARED_CONFIG provider MUST NOT reload or overwrite it; downstream providers read from it.

AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID.description: The value of the AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID environment variable

AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY.description: The value of the AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY environment variable

AWS_SESSION_TOKEN.description: The value of the AWS_SESSION_TOKEN environment variable

AWS_ACCOUNT_ID.description: The value of the AWS_ACCOUNT_ID environment variable

AWS_WEB_IDENTITY_TOKEN_FILE.description: The value of the AWS_WEB_IDENTITY_TOKEN_FILE environment variable

AWS_ROLE_ARN.description: The value of the AWS_ROLE_ARN environment variable

AWS_CONTAINER_CREDENTIALS_FULL_URI.description: The value of the AWS_CONTAINER_CREDENTIALS_FULL_URI environment variable

AWS_CONTAINER_CREDENTIALS_RELATIVE_URI.description: The value of the AWS_CONTAINER_CREDENTIALS_RELATIVE_URI environment variable

AWS_PROFILE.description: The value of the AWS_PROFILE environment variable

configFiles.description: The contents of configuration files relevant to resolving AWS credentials

aws.description: The combined contents of the AWS config and credentials files

stsResponse.description: The simulated response of the STS call the chain would make for an assume-role / web-identity source. Requires aws-credentials-sts.

accessKeyId.description: The AWS access key ID returned by STS

secretAccessKey.description: The AWS secret access key returned by STS

sessionToken.description: The AWS session token returned by STS

accountId.description: The AWS account ID returned by STS

error.description: The error raised by the STS call

ssoResponse.description: The simulated response of the SSO call the chain would make. Requires aws-credentials-sso.

accessKeyId.description: The AWS access key ID returned by SSO

secretAccessKey.description: The AWS secret access key returned by SSO

sessionToken.description: The AWS session token returned by SSO

accountId.description: The AWS account ID returned by SSO

token.description: The bearer token returned by SSO (for TokenIdentity)

error.description: The error raised by the SSO call

processResponse.description: The simulated response of the credential_process invocation.

accessKeyId.description: The AWS access key ID returned by the process

secretAccessKey.description: The AWS secret access key returned by the process

sessionToken.description: The AWS session token returned by the process

accountId.description: The AWS account ID returned by the process

error.description: The error raised by the process

httpResponse.description: The simulated response of the ECS/container HTTP endpoint. Requires aws-credentials-ecs.

accessKeyId.description: The AWS access key ID returned by HTTP

secretAccessKey.description: The AWS secret access key returned by HTTP

sessionToken.description: The AWS session token returned by HTTP

accountId.description: The AWS account ID returned by HTTP

error.description: The error raised by the HTTP call

imdsResponse.description: The simulated response of the IMDS call. Requires aws-credentials-imds. IMDS is the only non-terminal built-in source; it may fail through to a lower-priority provider.

accessKeyId.description: The AWS access key ID returned by IMDS

secretAccessKey.description: The AWS secret access key returned by IMDS

sessionToken.description: The AWS session token returned by IMDS

accountId.description: The AWS account ID returned by IMDS

error.description: The error message from the IMDS call

loginToken.description: The cached login_session credentials for the active profile. Requires aws-credentials-login.

accessKeyId.description: The cached AWS access key ID

secretAccessKey.description: The cached AWS secret access key

invalidateAndResolveAgain.description: When true, the harness resolves once, calls invalidate() on the chain, then resolves a second time. Source responses whose value is an array are interpreted per-attempt (index 0 for the first resolve, index 1 for the second). 'expected' describes the result of the SECOND resolve. Defaults to false.

expected.description: The expected outcome. Exactly one of 'resolved', 'assemblyError', or 'resolutionError' is populated. 'assembledOrder', 'selectedProvider', and 'featureIds' are optional assertions layered on top.

resolved.description: The identity the chain resolves. null when resolution fails (see resolutionError).

accessKeyId.description: The resolved AWS access key ID

secretAccessKey.description: The resolved AWS secret access key

selectedProvider.description: The canonical name() of the provider whose resolver produced 'resolved'. Asserts precedence/ordering, not just the credential values.

featureIds.description: The feature IDs the winning provider contributes to the User-Agent.

assembledOrder.description: The canonical names of the resolvers in the assembled chain, in order. Optional assertion used by ordering tests. Assembly stops after a terminal resolver, so this list ends at the first terminal provider.

assemblyError.description: Populated when client construction MUST fail during chain assembly (e.g. duplicate name, duplicate Standard slot claim).

conflict.description: The name or slot that collided

resolutionError.description: Populated when resolution fails after all resolvers were tried (or the chain was empty). The chain returns a single aggregated error.

providersTried.description: The canonical names of every provider the chain attempted, in order. Empty when the assembled chain had no resolvers.

moduleSuggestions.description: Dependency coordinates for any detected-but-unclaimed slot, included in the aggregated error to keep it actionable.
